item.name: Auth
item.description: Unauthenticated IdP — login / refresh / logout / JWKS (per-user ES256 tokens).

item.name: Exchange email + password for an access + refresh token

request.name: Exchange email + password for an access + refresh token

description.content:
The credential IS the auth — unauthenticated. On success returns a short-lived
ES256 access token (Bearer for `/api/*`), a rotating opaque refresh token (returned
ONCE), and the user's flat permission list. The error is a GENERIC 401 for both an
unknown email and a wrong password (no account enumeration); a disabled account is
403; too many failed attempts is 429 with `Retry-After`.

response.name: Invalid email or password (generic — no enumeration).

item.name: Rotate a refresh token for a fresh access + refresh pair

request.name: Rotate a refresh token for a fresh access + refresh pair

description.content:
The presented refresh token is REVOKED and a successor is issued in the same family
(rotation). Replaying a rotated-out token is reuse-detection → the whole family is
revoked and the call 401s. A disabled user cannot refresh. Permissions are
re-resolved, so a `perm_version`-driven change reaches the new access token.

response.name: A rotated token pair (same shape as login).

item.name: Revoke the presented refresh token's whole family

request.name: Revoke the presented refresh token's whole family

description.content:
Idempotent — an unknown / already-revoked token still returns 204 (logout never
confirms a token's validity). Revokes the entire rotation family.

response.name: Logged out (empty body) — always, even for an unknown token.

description.content:
Public key material for verifying the access token (the pipeline verifies
internally; this exists for external verifiers + the live smoke test). Public-only
(never the private scalar). `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300`.

item.name: Public feed
item.description: Unauthenticated read-only `/v1/*` surface (landing page / public consumers).

description.content:
Returns only `published` deals as the curated `PublicDeal` DTO. Sort is
reliability-blended (the raw reliability score is never exposed): the primary
key decides order, ties break by source reliability then id.
Exact service name match (case-sensitive), e.g. `Disney+`.
ISO country (market-driven; DE for v1). (This can only be one of DE)

Max true monthly cost (EUR), inclusive.
Default `cost_asc`. (This can only be one of cost_asc,verified_desc)
Page size. Default 20, hard-capped at 100.

item.name: Candidates
item.description: Review queue — list, edit, approve, reject, audit. (admin)

description.content:
Lowest-confidence first. Defaults to the reviewable pair (`candidate` +
`in_review`) when `status` is omitted. Requires a valid token (Auth/IAM Phase 2 —
all `/api/*` reads now require auth). Each item's `evidence` carries
`evidence_screenshot_url` / `evidence_html_url` / `evidence_terms_url` pointing at
the gated `GET /api/evidence/:id/:artifact` endpoint (ACR-13).
(This can only be one of candidate,in_review,published,expired,rejected)
Inclusive upper bound on the stored confidence (0–1).

response.name: Candidates, each joined with its evidence bundle.

description.content: (This can only be one of candidate,in_review,published,expired,rejected)
Inclusive upper bound on the stored confidence (0–1).

(This can only be one of candidate,in_review,published,expired,rejected)

item.name: Aggregate review-queue counts for the view-cards + filter rail (ACR-5)

request.name: Aggregate review-queue counts for the view-cards + filter rail (ACR-5)

description.content:
One call returns the queue's headline counts: `all_pending` (reviewable
deals), `low_confidence` (confidence ≤ 0.5), `human_edited`, a per-route
breakdown, and `rejected_today` (rejections on the current UTC day, from the
reviews audit log — a true date-bounded count). Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

The share of the pending review queue (`candidate` + `in_review`) in each age
band, where age is `now − evidence.captured_at`. The three bands are always
returned, summing to 100 (an empty queue returns all three at 0). Backs the
Dashboard "Queue freshness" card. Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Edit a candidate's reviewer-correctable fields (before approve)

request.name: Edit a candidate's reviewer-correctable fields (before approve)

description.content:
Applies an allowlisted patch via the pure `applyCandidatePatch`. Re-validates,
tags every changed field path into the deal's `human_edited`, keeps the model
grounding quotes, and re-derives `true_cost_monthly` on a price change. Status
stays `candidate`. Identity / provenance / status are never editable.
(Required) Resource UUID. A non-UUID currently yields 500 (see docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md).

item.name: Evidence
item.description: Bearer-gated reviewer fetch of evidence artifact bytes (screenshot/html/terms). (admin)

item.name: Fetch one evidence artifact's bytes (Bearer-gated, reviewer-only)

request.name: Fetch one evidence artifact's bytes (Bearer-gated, reviewer-only)

description.content: Stream the raw bytes of ONE artifact (screenshot / archived HTML / verbatim terms text) of an evidence bundle. This is the AUTHENTICATED complement of the screenshot-only public CDN: the public CDN exposes only `screenshot.png` (it 403s html/terms), so a reviewer reaches the full bundle here instead. The admin `AdminEvidence` URLs (`evidence_*_url`) point at this path. Unlike the other read endpoints this GET REQUIRES the bearer — evidence bytes (raw HTML + verbatim copyrighted terms) are sensitive. Responses carry `Cache-Control: private, no-store`. `evidence.json` (metadata) is deliberately NOT exposed (it duplicates `GET /api/candidates`); an unknown artifact kind (or a non-UUID id) is a 404.
(Required) Resource UUID. A non-UUID currently yields 500 (see docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md).
(Required) Which artifact of the bundle to stream. (This can only be one of screenshot,html,terms)

response.name: The artifact bytes, with the stored content-type.

description.content: (Required) Resource UUID. A non-UUID currently yields 500 (see docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md).

item.name: Create a candidate from an ad-hoc manual capture (no backing task, ACR-12)

request.name: Create a candidate from an ad-hoc manual capture (no backing task, ACR-12)

description.content:
For a deal a reviewer captures from scratch — NOT a crawler "couldn't read"
task. Mints a `done` `ad_hoc` capture task AND an evidence-backed `candidate`
in one call. Same trust contract as `/{id}/complete`: evidence REQUIRED (by
reference), source_url pinned from the evidence, whole record `human_edited`,
never auto-published. Distinct from `/{id}/complete`, which needs a task id.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.description: Cross-deal audit feed of recent review decisions. (admin)

item.name: Cross-deal audit feed of recent review decisions (ACR-7)

request.name: Cross-deal audit feed of recent review decisions (ACR-7)

description.content:
Recent human review decisions across all deals, newest first — backs the
Dashboard "recent activity" card and the Audit-log screen. Only the persisted
review actions appear (`approve` · `reject` · `edit`); `promote`/`extract` are
not yet recorded as audit rows (deferred). Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
Filter to one reviewer identity (approver).
Filter to one entity (deal id).
Only decisions at/after this ISO-8601 instant.
Filter to one reviewer identity (approver).
Filter to one entity (deal id).
Only decisions at/after this ISO-8601 instant.

Reviewer registry — the pipeline is the system of record for reviewer
identity. Each member's `review_count` is DERIVED from the reviews audit log
(by email = approver), so it never drifts. Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Update the signed-in reviewer's own profile — name and/or own password (ACR-11 + Phase 3)

request.name: Update the signed-in reviewer's own profile — name and/or own password (ACR-11 + Phase 3)

description.content:
SELF-service, keyed by the caller's TOKEN email (the request carries no `approver`) — a
user can only ever edit their OWN row. `name` edits the display name. `currentPassword`
+ `newPassword` (all-or-nothing) change the caller's OWN password: the current-password
proof IS the authorization (no `team:manage` needed, unlike the admin reset on
`/api/users/{id}`), a wrong current password is a 401, and a successful change bumps the
user's `token_version` — logging out every other live session (the panel should
re-login). At least one of `name` or the password pair must be present. Bearer-gated.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Empty patch, a password pair that isn't all-or-nothing, a new password failing the policy, or no member for this caller.

item.name: Update a user — role / status / password / name (Phase 3)

request.name: Update a user — role / status / password / name (Phase 3)

description.content:
Change a user's `role`, `status` (active|disabled), or `password` — each gated
`team:manage`. A user may edit their OWN `name` without `team:manage` (the self
path); any privileged sub-field, or editing another user, requires `team:manage`.
Changing role/status/password bumps the user's `token_version` (immediate revoke);
disabling also revokes their refresh tokens. The LAST-ADMIN GUARD refuses to disable
or demote the last active holder of `roles:manage`/`team:manage` (409).
(Required) Resource UUID. A non-UUID currently yields 500 (see docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md).
(Required) Resource UUID. A non-UUID currently yields 500 (see docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

description.content:
Replace the role's permission set and/or edit its description. A built-in
`is_system` role's permission SET is immutable (409 on a permissions edit); its
description may still be edited. A permission-set change bumps `perm_version`.
Gated `roles:manage`.
(Required) The role NAME (a free-form string key, not a UUID).
(Required) The role NAME (a free-form string key, not a UUID).

item.name: Permissions
item.description: The permission catalogue + the caller's own effective permissions. (admin)

description.content:
Every permission key (so the panel role editor can render the catalogue without the
enum shipping to the client). Gated `roles:manage`.

Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

The authenticated caller's role + effective permission set (from the verified
token). Any valid token — no named permission required. The panel uses it to gate
page access (cosmetic; the pipeline is the real gate).

item.name: Alerts
item.description: Persisted alerts — list, acknowledge, resolve (source reliability / budget). (admin)

item.name: List persisted alerts + the open count (ACR-8)

request.name: List persisted alerts + the open count (ACR-8)

description.content:
Recent alerts (newest first) with their EFFECTIVE status — a budget alert from
a past UTC day and a reliability alert whose source has recovered auto-resolve
at read time; a manual ack/resolve always wins. `open_count` feeds the top-bar
bell badge. Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Metrics
item.description: Dashboard rollups — today's throughput, queue freshness, KPIs/cost/confidence. (admin)

description.content:
The share of the pending review queue (`candidate` + `in_review`) in each age
band, where age is `now − evidence.captured_at`. The three bands are always
returned, summing to 100 (an empty queue returns all three at 0). Backs the
Dashboard "Queue freshness" card. Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Counts of today's (UTC) approve/reject/edit decisions plus the mean
capture→decision latency (`avg_review_seconds`, or null when no decision today
has a resolvable capture time). Backs the Dashboard "Today's throughput" card.
Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
The window. Only `today` is supported in v1; any other value is a 400. (This can only be one of today)
The window. Only `today` is supported in v1; any other value is a 400. (This can only be one of today)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
The window. Only `today` is supported in v1; any other value is a 400. (This can only be one of today)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Metrics screen rollup — KPIs / cost-per-day / confidence (ACR-10 Metrics)

request.name: Metrics screen rollup — KPIs / cost-per-day / confidence (ACR-10 Metrics)

description.content:
KPI cards, the last 14 UTC days of crawl cost, and the pending-queue confidence
distribution — all derived from real pipeline data (`crawl_runs` + the `reviews`
log + the pending `deals`). Backs the Metrics screen. Requires a valid token.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Settings
item.description: Operational settings — read the knobs; PATCH the writable ones (env mirrors are read-only). (admin)

description.content:
The grouped settings view: panel-editable knobs PLUS read-only env/derived
mirrors (each row carries `read_only`). A writable knob shows its stored override
if set, else the live config value. Requires a valid token (Phase 2).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Set a writable knob's override (`affiliate_disclosure`, `daily_budget_queued`).
`affiliate_disclosure` takes effect immediately; `daily_budget_queued` is stamped
with the current deployment and applies on the NEXT deploy, then self-clears (the
in-effect `daily_budget` is read-only). A read-only or unknown key is a 409; an
invalid value is a 400.
(Required)
(Required)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
